title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy scalp
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy scalp are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to alleviate scalp irritation and inflammation. They are a natural and effective solution for itchy scalp, offering relief from discomfort and improving overall scalp health. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with itchy scalp include tea tree, rosemary, and lavender. Tea tree tincture has antifungal and antiseptic properties that combat scalp infections, while rosemary tincture stimulates blood flow and promotes hair growth. Lavender tincture soothes the scalp, reducing inflammation and providing a calming effect. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into a hair care routine, individuals can experience a significant reduction in itchiness, leading to improved scalp health, less discomfort, and enhanced self-confidence.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe inflammation and fight against scalp irritations. The herb's active compounds, such as sesquiterpine lactones and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy scalp. Moreover, yarrow tincture can help regulate sebum production, which can prevent the buildup of oil and dead skin cells that can lead to itchiness and dandruff. Its astringent properties can also help to tighten the scalp's pores, reducing the risk of irritants and bacteria entering the scalp. Overall, yarrow tincture can provide relief from itchy scalp and promote a healthy scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy scalp as itchiness is often caused by inflammation of the scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Yarrow tincture has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, preventing infections that can cause itchiness. The active constituents responsible for this property include alpha-pinene, sabinene, and beta-pinene.

Astringent: Yarrow tincture has astringent properties that help tighten and tone the scalp, reducing excess oil production that can lead to itchiness. The active constituents responsible for this property include tannins and resins.

Analgesic: Yarrow tincture has analgesic properties that help relieve pain and discomfort, which is important for itchy scalp as scratching can cause pain and irritation. The active constituents responsible for this property include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Yarrow tincture helps improve blood flow to the scalp, which is important for itchy scalp as increased blood flow can help promote healing and reduce inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh yarrow plant or 1 ounce of dried yarrow into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the yarrow, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, amber glass bottle and apply several drops to the scalp as needed to relieve itching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, yarrow flowers help soothe itchy scalp and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Containing azulene, yarrow leaves have a calming effect on the scalp, providing relief from itchiness and irritation.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, yarrow seeds nourish the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and alleviating itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal an irritated scalp. Burdock is rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which can help to nourish and moisturize the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. Additionally, burdock has been shown to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and relieving itchiness. Its natural detoxifying properties can also help to remove impurities and buildup from the scalp, providing relief from itchiness caused by product residue or environmental pollutants. Overall, burdock tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for an itchy scalp, promoting a healthy and balanced scalp environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock tincture helps reduce itchiness on the scalp by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of essential fatty acids and tannins.
Antimicrobial: Burdock tincture combats bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchiness, due to its active constituents like arctiin and lignans.

Diuretic: Burdock tincture promotes scalp health by increasing the elimination of toxins and excess fluids, which can alleviate itching, as a result of its diuretic properties from potassium and asparagin.
Antipruritic: Burdock tincture has a soothing effect on itchy skin, directly addressing the main symptom of an itchy scalp, due to its antipruritic compounds like tannins and essential oils.

Nutritive: Burdock tincture provides essential nutrients for hair and scalp health, reducing itchiness by nourishing the skin and promoting a healthy scalp environment, attributed to its rich content of vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried burdock root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the burdock root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Label the bottle with the ingredient, date, and recommended dosage (20-30 drops in a small amount of water or directly on the scalp, up to three times a day) and store in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and promote hair growth.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions, including itchy scalp, due to their soothing and cooling effects.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain essential fatty acids and phytosterols that can help nourish the scalp, reduce inflammation, and alleviate itchiness.
Buds: Burdock buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal an irritated scalp. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, can reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief from itching. Additionally, pot marigold tincture can help eliminate scalp-dwelling microbes that may contribute to itchiness and other scalp issues. Its nourishing and moisturizing properties can also help restore the scalp's natural balance, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing the likelihood of itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is important for itchy scalp as it can alleviate symptoms of skin irritation and redness. This property is attributed to the active constituents, including flavonoids and triterpenes.

Antimicrobial: The tincture has antimicrobial properties that help combat scalp infections, which can cause itching. Active constituents responsible for this property include the antimicrobial alkaloids and essential oils found in pot marigold.

Anti-pruritic: Pot marigold tincture has been shown to have an anti-pruritic effect, which helps alleviate itching sensations. This property is essential for treating itchy scalp, and it is mainly due to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds and flavonoids.

Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing, which is crucial for treating itchy scalp conditions such as dermatitis or psoriasis. Active constituents responsible for this property include the flavonoids, triterpenes, and carotenoids present in pot marigold.

Hydrating: Pot marigold tincture has hydrating properties that help maintain the skin's natural moisture barrier. This is important for itchy scalp as it prevents dryness and further irritation. The hydrating effect is primarily due to the presence of mucilaginous compounds and saponins found in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, gently rinse, and let dry for 24 hours.
Chop dried flowers into small pieces, fill a clean jar halfway, and add 80-proof vodka until flowers are fully submerged.
Seal the jar, store in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Apply a few drops of the marigold tincture to your scalp, massaging gently, and let it sit for 30 minutes before washing your hair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for soothing an itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have been found to contain compounds with antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for treating itchy scalp.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a great source of fatty acids, which can help to moisturize and reduce inflammation on an itchy scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and heal an irritated scalp. The tincture is made from the flowers of the German chamomile plant, which contain active compounds like azulene and chamazulene that are known to reduce inflammation and irritation. These compounds also have a calming effect on the skin, which can help to alleviate the discomfort and itchiness associated with dry or inflamed scalp conditions. Additionally, German chamomile tincture can help to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dandruff and flakiness. Overall, the soothing and healing properties of German chamomile tincture make it an effective natural remedy for treating an itchy scalp.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate itching and irritation on the scalp. This property is due to the active constituent, chamazulene, that provides the tincture with its anti-inflammatory effects.

Antimicrobial: Chamomile tincture has antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria or fungi on the scalp, reducing the risk of infections that can cause itching. The antimicrobial effects are attributed to the presence of active compounds like alpha-bisabolol and matricin.

Anti-pruritic: German chamomile tincture has been shown to have an anti-pruritic effect, which means it can help alleviate itching. This property is particularly important for treating itchy scalp, as it directly addresses the primary symptom. The anti-pruritic effect is attributed to the chamazulene content in the tincture.

Antioxidant: Chamomile tincture contains antioxidants, which can help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for maintaining overall scalp health and reducing inflammation that can lead to itching. The antioxidant properties are due to the presence of various flavonoids and terpenoids, including apigenin and luteolin.

Wound healing: German chamomile tincture has wound-healing properties, which can help soothe and heal irritated skin on the scalp. This property is important for treating itchy scalp, as it promotes skin regeneration and reduces the risk of further irritation. The wound-healing effects are attributed to the active constituents, such as bisabolol and matricin, which have been shown to promote tissue regeneration and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh German chamomile flowers, ideally on a sunny day when the flowers are fully open.
Coarsely chop the flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, then pour 10 ounces of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the flowers.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
After the extraction period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth, squeezing out the marc (solid plant material) to extract all liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, in a cool, dark place; to use, dilute 1-2 ml in water and apply directly to the scalp, massaging gently before rinsing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of German chamomile are rich in flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them ideal for soothing an itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves of German chamomile contain tannins and volatile oils that can help reduce inflammation and irritation, providing relief for itchy scalp.
Seeds: German chamomile seeds are a source of essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help nourish and protect the scalp, reducing itchiness and promoting healthy hair growth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation. Chicory is rich in vitamins and minerals that promote healthy hair growth and can help to balance the pH level of the scalp. It also contains inulin, a prebiotic that can help to maintain a healthy scalp microbiome, reducing the risk of irritation and itching. The tincture form of chicory allows for easy absorption and quick relief of itchy scalp symptoms. Regular use of chicory tincture can help to prevent itchy scalp and promote healthy, shiny hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chicory tincture helps reduce itchiness and inflammation on the scalp by inhibiting the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thanks to its active constituents like sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Chicory tincture inhibits the growth of microorganisms that can cause scalp irritation and itchiness, due to the presence of antimicrobial compounds such as lactones and polyphenols.
Antipruritic: Chicory tincture alleviates itching by reducing histamine release and inflammation, which is facilitated by its active constituents, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.

Astringent: Chicory tincture tightens skin cells and reduces sebum production, thereby preventing a buildup of oils and dead skin cells that can cause itchiness; this is attributed to its tannin content.

Soothing: Chicory tincture has a cooling and soothing effect on the scalp, which can help alleviate itchiness and irritation; this property is due to the presence of various polyphenols and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried chicory root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the chicory root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture, discarding the solids, and store the liquid in a tightly sealed glass container.
Apply the tincture to your scalp as needed, massaging it in gently and allowing it to sit for 15-20 minutes before rinsing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots contain anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them ideal for soothing itchy scalp.
Flowers: Chicory flowers have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, helping to alleviate itchiness and irritation on the scalp.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils, chicory seeds can help moisturize and soothe a dry, itchy scalp.
Leaves: Chicory leaves contain tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent properties that can help reduce scalp itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with itchy scalp because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and reduce irritation. The herb is rich in silica and other minerals that can help strengthen hair follicles, improve scalp circulation, and promote hair growth. Horsetail tincture can also help balance the pH level of the scalp, reducing the production of excess oil and dandruff that can cause itching. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and inflammation, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Regular use of horsetail tincture can help maintain a healthy scalp and promote strong, shiny hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce itching and redness on the scalp, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help control scalp conditions like dandruff, caused by bacterial or fungal infections, thanks to the antiseptic compound equisetin.
Astringent: Horsetail tincture's astringent properties help tighten skin cells, reducing scalp irritation and itching, due to its high silica and tannin content.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of horsetail tincture help protect the scalp from damage and inflammation, which can lead to itching, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Circulation-boosting: Horsetail tincture promotes blood flow to the scalp, encouraging hair growth and reducing itching, due to the presence of silica and saponins, which help strengthen blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried horsetail herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle and use as needed, diluted in water, to soothe an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain silica and other minerals that can help strengthen hair follicles and reduce itchiness on the scalp.
Stems: Horsetail stems have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp and promote healthy hair growth.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce inflammation and relieve itching on the scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate irritation and reduce inflammation. The active compound in licorice, glycyrrhizin, helps to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, providing relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, licorice tincture can help to balance the pH of the scalp, creating an unfavorable environment for fungal growth, which can often be a cause of itchy scalp. Its emollient properties also help to moisturize and protect the scalp, preventing dryness and further reducing itchiness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituent glycyrrhizin, helps reduce itchiness and redness on the scalp caused by inflammation.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of licorice tincture, attributed to its flavonoids, aids in preventing and treating scalp infections that can cause itchiness.
Antipruritic: Licorice tincture's antipruritic property, resulting from its active compound liquiritin, helps alleviate itching sensations, providing relief for itchy scalp.
Demulcent: The demulcent property of licorice tincture, due to its mucilage content, soothes and protects the scalp by forming a barrier against irritants, reducing itchiness.
Anti-allergic: Licorice tincture's anti-allergic property, attributed to its flavonoids, helps inhibit histamine release, reducing allergic reactions on the scalp that can lead to itching.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe itchy scalp.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate itchiness and promote a healthy scalp.
Seeds: Licorice seeds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating itchy scalp.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have soothing properties, reducing itchiness on the scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing itchiness and irritation. Its natural tannins help to remove excess oil and impurities from the scalp, while its high antioxidant content helps to protect the scalp from damage and promote healing. Witch hazel is also gentle and non-irritating, making it a safe and effective natural remedy for itchy scalp. Its soothing and calming effects provide quick relief, leaving the scalp feeling refreshed and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce itching and redness on the scalp by constricting blood vessels and inhibiting the release of inflammatory agents. This is mainly due to the presence of tannins and gallic acid.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help to remove excess oil and impurities from the scalp, reducing itching and irritation. This is attributed to the high tannin content.

Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of bacteria and fungi on the scalp, which can cause itching and other skin issues. The primary active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, such as eugenol and hexenol.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of witch hazel tincture help protect the scalp from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itching and irritation. The high concentration of polyphenols, such as gallic acid and catechins, provides this antioxidant effect.

Soothing: Witch hazel tincture has a soothing effect on the scalp, which can help alleviate itching and irritation. This is primarily due to the astringent and anti-inflammatory properties of tannins and volatile oils.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried witch hazel bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped bark, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
After the allotted time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the contents and date, and store in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp.

Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant are rich in polyphenols and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate itching and irritation on the scalp.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness and inflammation on the scalp.
Twigs: The twigs of the witch hazel plant are rich in tannins and essential oils, which have astringent and antiseptic properties that can help relieve itching and promote scalp health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe inflammation and reduce irritation. The herb contains active compounds such as hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have calming effects on the skin. When applied to the scalp, St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate itching, reduce redness, and promote overall scalp health. Its healing properties can also help to combat dryness, dandruff, and other conditions that can contribute to an itchy scalp. By nourishing and protecting the scalp, St. John's wort tincture can provide relief and promote healthy hair growth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tincture reduces inflammation, which is important for itchy scalp as inflammation can exacerbate the itching sensation. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin and hyperforin.
Antimicrobial: The tincture helps combat bacterial and fungal infections, which can cause itchy scalp. The main active constituents responsible are hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic: St. John's wort tincture can help alleviate pain, including the discomfort caused by an itchy scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.

Wound-healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is important for itchy scalp as it can help soothe irritated skin and prevent further infection. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and tannins.

Antipruritic: St. John's wort tincture has antipruritic properties, which means it can help alleviate itching. This is especially important for itchy scalp as it directly addresses the main symptom. The active constituents responsible for this property are hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean, dry jar about 3/4 full.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the herbs until they are completely submerged, covering with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, discarding the spent herbs and reserving the liquid.
Store the finished St. John's Wort tincture in a labeled, amber glass bottle, using 1-2 ml as needed to soothe an itchy scalp.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate itchy scalp.
Leaves: Contain similar active compounds as flowers, helping to reduce inflammation and irritation, promoting a healthy scalp.
Stems: Although less potent than flowers and leaves, stems still contain active compounds that can help soothe an itchy scalp.
Seeds: Seeds may have lower concentrations of active compounds, but they can still contribute to the overall effectiveness of a St. John's Wort tincture for itchy scalp.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with itchy scalp because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and itching. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to have calming effects on the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy scalp. Additionally, lavender tincture can help to balance the pH level of the scalp, creating an unfavorable environment for fungal growth, which can often be a cause of itchy scalp. By promoting scalp health and reducing inflammation, English lavender tincture can provide relief for an itchy scalp and support overall hair and scalp health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate itching and soothe an itchy scalp. This property is due to the presence of linalyl acetate and linalool.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help combat scalp infections, which can cause itching. The primary active constituents responsible for this are eucalyptol, camphor, and lavandulyl acetate.

Analgesic: English lavender tincture has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort associated with an itchy scalp. The main active constituents responsible for this are linalool and eucalyptol.

Sedative: The sedative properties of English lavender tincture can help reduce itching by promoting relaxation and reducing stress. The primary active constituents responsible for this are linalool and lavandulol.

Wound healing: Its wound-healing properties can help repair any damage to the scalp, promoting overall skin health and reducing itching. The primary active constituents responsible for this are linalool, caryophyllene, and terpinen-4-ol.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried English lavender flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the lavender, ensuring all flowers are submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent lavender.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in soothing compounds, the flowers of English lavender are used to make medicinal tinctures for itchy scalp due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Containing high levels of calming agents, lavender leaves are used in tinctures for itchy scalp to help alleviate irritation and reduce redness.
Buds: Packed with essential oils, lavender buds are used in medicinal tinctures for itchy scalp to promote scalp health and soothe inflammation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy scalp with herbal tinctures is by using a solution of calendula, chamomile, and lavender. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can alleviate itchiness and irritation. Mix equal parts of these tinctures, dilute with water, and apply directly onto the scalp. Gently massage for a few minutes, allowing the solution to penetrate. Leave it on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing. Repeat this process 2-3 times a week for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy scalp that are treated with herbal tinctures include dandruff, seborrheic dermatitis, and psoriasis. These conditions can cause symptoms such as itching, flaking, and redness of the scalp. Herbal tinctures, which are concentrated extracts of herbs, can be used to alleviate these symptoms. For example, tea tree oil and rosemary tinctures have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy, inflamed scalp. Additionally, calendula tincture has been shown to have wound-healing effects, which can help reduce scalp irritation. As with any treatment, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal tinctures to ensure safety and effectiveness.
